title: Math Training
description: Train your number sense and problem-solving!

scenes.speech: Welcome to the Math Dojo! Let's explore the world of numbers! 🔢
scenes.title: Math Dojo
scenes.content: In math training, what matters is not only calculation speed but also the skill of turning a problem into a 'picture' or familiar example. Before memorizing formulas, learn to explain in words what you're solving.

scenes.title: Picture the Problem
scenes.content: Word problems look hard as text. Sketch the people and things in them, and you'll see whether to add or subtract. Try writing it out by hand.
scenes.highlight:
Rephrase steps:
1. Write the items
2. Connect them with arrows
3. Circle what you need to find

scenes.speech: Asking 'roughly how much?' helps catch mistakes fast!
scenes.title: Estimation Training
scenes.content: Before calculating, predict 'about how big the answer should be'. If you misplace a digit, you'll notice instantly. Build a feel for hundreds and thousands.

scenes.speech: Memorize formulas together with 'when to use them'!
scenes.title: Formula Training
scenes.content: Each formula belongs to a setting — area, speed, ratio. Pair the formula with its scene so that just seeing the numbers reminds you which one applies.
scenes.highlight:
Pair memory:
- Speed = distance ÷ time
- Area = length × width
- Ratio = compared / base

scenes.speech: Make a 'mistake notebook'! No same mistake twice!
scenes.title: Mistake Notebook
scenes.content: Record problems you got wrong with the cause and the fix. Sort causes into 'careless', 'method unknown', 'misread' so countermeasures fit each.
scenes.highlight:
3 mistake types:
- Careless
- Method unknown
- Misread

scenes.speech: Today's challenge: 'turn things around you into numbers'!
scenes.title: Find the Numbers
scenes.content: Find three things at home — count of X, length of Y, weight of Z — and write them down. Math is everywhere outside the textbook.
scenes.highlight:
Finds:
- Kitchen plates count
- Wall length
- Bottle weight

scenes.speech: Getting hints from AI is OK! Ask for the path, not the answer!
scenes.title: AI and Math
scenes.content: For tough problems, ask the AI: 'don't show the answer, just walk me through the steps'. Receiving hints little by little lets you finish on your own.
scenes.highlight:
How to ask AI:
- Hide the answer
- Just the first hint
- Explain with a diagram

scenes.speech: Great training! Friends with numbers yet? 🌟
scenes.title: Training Complete!
scenes.content: The real power of math is being able to explain your reasoning, not just produce an answer. Share one of today's numbers with your family.
